source_alignment.subject: Science
source_alignment.source_statement: Identify and describe the functions of different parts of flowering plants: roots, stem/trunk, leaves and flowers.
source_alignment.derived_objective_note: This pack teaches plant-part function using an interactive grow-and-test simulation rather than static labelling alone.

objective.child_goal: I can explain what roots, stems, leaves and flowers do.
objective.statement: Identify plant parts and explain how each part helps a flowering plant live and reproduce.
misconceptions: Thinks roots only hold the plant still and do not take in water.
Thinks leaves are decorative rather than food-making parts.
Confuses flower function with leaf function.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The plant part that takes in water and helps hold the plant in place.
vocabulary.audio_script: Roots take in water from the soil and anchor the plant.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Animated roots drawing blue water drops from soil.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The plant part that supports the plant and carries water.
vocabulary.audio_script: The stem holds the plant up and carries water to other parts.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A glowing water path moving up the stem.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Plant parts that help the plant make food.
vocabulary.audio_script: Leaves use light to help the plant make food.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Leaves catching sunlight and making energy sparkles.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The plant part that helps make seeds.
vocabulary.audio_script: Flowers help many plants make seeds.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A flower opening to reveal seed icons.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: What does this plant need to grow well?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Reactivate basic plant needs before connecting parts to functions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Plants need things like water and light. Their parts help them get what they need.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A small plant changes when water or light icons are added.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Open the greenhouse scanner to see each plant part doing a job.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce the idea that plant parts have functions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Every part of a plant has a job.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A scanner highlights roots, stem, leaves and flower in sequence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Watch water move from roots up the stem.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model root and stem functions as connected systems.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: The roots take in water. The stem carries it upwards.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Blue water dots move from soil through roots and up the stem.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Drag each job card to the plant part that does it.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Link vocabulary, visual part and function.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Read or listen to the job, then match it to the plant part.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A labelled plant with draggable function cards.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: What happens if the roots cannot take in water?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check causal reasoning rather than memorised labels.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Predict what will happen, then run the greenhouse test.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A simulation blocks root water intake and shows the plant wilting.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Sol why leaves are not just decoration.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to repair leaf-function misconceptions.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I thought leaves were just pretty. Can you show me their job?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion points at leaves while sunlight appears.

manipulatives.purpose: Show plant parts as working systems through cause-and-effect tests.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners scan parts, toggle light/water, block functions and match job cards to plant parts.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: All visual effects have audio labels; reduced-motion uses static before-and-after panels.

manipulatives.purpose: Separate identifying a plant part from explaining the evidence-based job that part performs.
manipulatives.interaction_notes: Learners deliver root, stem, leaf and flower job cards to labelled greenhouse stations, inspect one evidence clue and reroute any card whose function does not match.
manipulatives.accessibility_notes: Keyboard move menus place each job card at a named plant part; single-switch scanning cycles through card, destination and check; static mode provides a two-column part-and-function matching table; reduced-motion mode removes delivery animation and reveals evidence as still diagrams with spoken and written descriptions.

practice.interleaving_notes: Interleave labels and functions only after learners can explain at least two part-function links.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Identify roots, stem, leaves and flowers from varied plant diagrams.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: plant image + four draggable labels + distractor label
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use varied flowering plants while keeping age-appropriate clarity.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Match plant parts to their functions using concise explanations.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: function description + plant part choices + misconception distractors
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Distractors should expose root, leaf and flower misunderstandings.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Predict and observe what happens when a plant part cannot do its job.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: blocked function + predict outcome + run simulation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep biological mechanism simple and accurate for Year 3.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Build scientific explanation using because, evidence and function language.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: scenario + choose best because sentence + evidence highlight
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Coordinate with English explanation sentence supports.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Provide quick spaced review across labels, functions and causal predictions.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: mixed part/function prompts from review scheduler
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep retrieval items short enough for daily warm-up.

body.prompt: What is one important job of the roots?
choices: Take in water from the soil
Make seeds
Catch sunlight to make food
Make petals bright

expected_answer.value: Take in water from the soil

hints: Look under the soil.
Watch where the water enters the plant.
question_variants.explanation: Roots help anchor the plant and take in water from the soil.

body.prompt: Drag the job 'helps the plant make food using light' to the correct plant part.
body.target_job: helps the plant make food using light
choices: roots
stem
leaves
flower

expected_answer.value: leaves

hints: Which part catches the sunlight?
The flat green parts do this job.
question_variants.explanation: Leaves help the plant make food using light.

body.prompt: Predict what will happen if roots cannot take in water.
choices: The plant may wilt because water cannot get in
The flower makes extra water
The leaves turn into roots
Nothing changes

expected_answer.value: The plant may wilt because water cannot get in

hints: What do roots take in?
What happens when the plant cannot get water?
question_variants.explanation: If roots cannot take in water, the plant may wilt because water cannot move through the plant.

body.prompt: Plant-part scanner 1: place the correct label on the highlighted structure.
body.highlighted_structure: fine branching structures spread through soil
body.location_clue: below the soil or growing medium
choices: roots
stem
trunk
leaves
body.narration_script: Place the roots label on the highlighted plant part.
body.supported_interaction: An adult or peer may read, scan, move the learner's named card or record an indicated explanation without supplying the science conclusion.
body.plant_part_model_route: Clear varied diagrams with named connection points, outline mode, concise alt descriptions and a two-column part/function table.
body.transport_sequence_route: Static water-drop sequence from growing medium to roots, stem/trunk and leaves with text labels; animation is optional.
body.evidence_comparison_route: Side-by-side before/after panels or simple tables with one changed condition and repeated observations.
body.investigation_planner_route: CHANGE, KEEP SAME and MEASURE/OBSERVE columns accept picture, text, pointing or adult-scribed cards.
body.sensory_safe_route: No sudden sounds, flashing, compulsory plant handling, strong smells or distressing wilt animation; tactile materials and real specimens are optional.
body.tactile_optional_route: Use raised plant outlines, pipe-cleaner stems or textured cards only by choice; complete visual, auditory and text routes remain available.
body.visual_route: Low-clutter plant model, generous spacing, persistent part labels and no function encoded by colour alone.
body.processing_route: Reveal one part, transport stage, variable or evidence row at a time; preserve correct work and allow repeated viewing.
body.motor_alternative: Tap, keyboard, switch scan, eye gaze, AAC, pointing or adult-scribed placement can replace dragging, speech and handwriting.
body.practical_safety: Teacher-led plant work; check allergies and sensitivities, do not taste plants or solutions, wash hands after handling soil or plant material.

gamification.reward: one greenhouse badge leaf for a checked model, sequence or fair plan

feedback.support_message: Labelling, sequencing, pointing, eye gaze, AAC, oral explanation and adult-scribed planning carry equal evidence; speed, handwriting and handling materials are not scored.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks roots only hold the plant still and do not take in water.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner selects anchoring only or predicts no effect when root water intake is blocked.
repair_sequence: Show water drops entering root hairs.
Trace water from roots into the stem.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner explains roots take in water in a new scenario.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks leaves are decorative rather than food-making parts.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner matches leaves to colour or decoration rather than food-making.
repair_sequence: Dim the sunlight and show the plant losing energy.
Turn sunlight back on and highlight leaves.
Ask learner to match the leaf job again.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner links leaves with light and food-making.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses flower function with leaf function.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner says flowers make food using light.
repair_sequence: Compare leaf and flower positions on the plant.
Animate leaves catching light and flowers making seeds.
Ask learner to sort two job cards.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner separates leaf and flower functions correctly.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use calm greenhouse colours and avoid wilting effects that feel punitive.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Offer static scan layers and before-after panels instead of flowing water animations.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Narrate each part name and function with replayable vocabulary cards.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Pair each function sentence with an icon and spoken explanation.
adaptive_support.attention_support: One test variable changes at a time in the simulation.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow learners to pause the scanner and inspect labels before answering.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use 'scientist test' language so wrong predictions become experiments.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach root, stem, leaf, flower, water, light, seed and because with visuals.

evidence.mastery_rule: Expected requires matching plant parts to functions; secure requires explaining simple cause-and-effect tests with evidence.
evidence.parent_summary: Can identify key parts of a flowering plant and explain the job each part does.
evidence.teacher_evidence: Links roots, stem, leaves and flowers to functions and uses simulation evidence to explain outcomes.
next_actions: Connect plant functions to life cycle and pollination after part functions are secure.
Use retrieval prompts with diagrams from varied plants.
Introduce simple investigation planning with one changed variable.

qa.notes: Review-stage Year 3 plant-functions pack with a deterministic 220-variant pilot bank. Three curated variants are unchanged. Generated tasks cover roots, stems/trunks, leaves, flowers, fruits and seeds; requirements for life and growth; water transport; pollination, seed formation and dispersal; age-appropriate cause/effect models; observation, diagrams, fair-test planning, evidence comparison, misconception repair and spaced transfer. Every generated task includes plant-part models, transport sequences, evidence comparisons or investigation planners, sensory-safe reduced-load SEND routes, optional tactile materials, alternative inputs, rich feedback and pressure-free garden-lab missions without timers, streaks, lives or loss. Selected narration references ElevenLabs assets held for human listening review; browser TTS is prohibited. Independent science, practical safety, accessibility, narration and renderer review remains required before promotion.
